verses.latin_vulgate: Psalmus David.   Domine, clamavi ad te : exaudi me ;  intende voci meæ, cum clamavero ad te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִזְמ֗וֹר לְדָ֫וִ֥ד יְהֹוָ֣ה קְ֭רָאתִיךָ ח֣וּשָׁה לִּ֑י הַאֲזִ֥ינָה ק֝וֹלִ֗י בְּקׇרְאִי־לָֽךְ׃
verses.king_james: Lord, I cry unto thee: make haste unto me; give ear unto my voice, when I cry unto thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dirigatur oratio mea sicut incensum in conspectu tuo ;  elevatio manuum mearum sacrificium vespertinum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תִּכּ֤וֹן תְּפִלָּתִ֣י קְטֹ֣רֶת לְפָנֶ֑יךָ מַֽשְׂאַ֥ת כַּ֝פַּ֗י מִנְחַת־עָֽרֶב׃
verses.king_james: Let my prayer be set forth before thee as incense; and the lifting up of my hands as the evening sacrifice.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pone, Domine, custodiam ori meo,  et ostium circumstantiæ labiis meis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שִׁיתָ֣ה יְ֭הֹוָה שׇׁמְרָ֣ה לְפִ֑י נִ֝צְּרָ֗ה עַל־דַּ֥ל שְׂפָתָֽי׃
verses.king_james: Set a watch, O Lord, before my mouth; keep the door of my lips.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non declines cor meum in verba malitiæ,  ad excusandas excusationes in peccatis ;  cum hominibus operantibus iniquitatem,  et non communicabo cum electis eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תַּט־לִבִּ֨י לְדָבָ֪ר ׀ רָ֡ע לְהִתְע֘וֹלֵ֤ל עֲלִל֨וֹת ׀ בְּרֶ֗שַׁע אֶת־אִישִׁ֥ים פֹּעֲלֵי־אָ֑וֶן וּבַל־אֶ֝לְחַ֗ם בְּמַנְעַמֵּיהֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: Incline not my heart to any evil thing, to practise wicked works with men that work iniquity: and let me not eat of their dainties.

verses.latin_vulgate: Corripiet me justus in misericordia, et increpabit me :  oleum autem peccatoris non impinguet caput meum.  Quoniam adhuc et oratio mea in beneplacitis eorum :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יֶ֥הֶלְמֵֽנִי־צַדִּ֨יק ׀ חֶ֡סֶד וְֽיוֹכִיחֵ֗נִי שֶׁ֣מֶן רֹ֭אשׁ אַל־יָנִ֣י רֹאשִׁ֑י כִּי־ע֥וֹד וּ֝תְפִלָּתִ֗י בְּרָעוֹתֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: Let the righteous smite me; it shall be a kindness: and let him reprove me; it shall be an excellent oil, which shall not break my head: for yet my prayer also shall be in their calamities.

verses.latin_vulgate: absorpti sunt juncti petræ judices eorum.  Audient verba mea, quoniam potuerunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נִשְׁמְט֣וּ בִֽידֵי־סֶ֭לַע שֹׁפְטֵיהֶ֑ם וְשָׁמְע֥וּ אֲ֝מָרַ֗י כִּ֣י נָעֵֽמוּ׃
verses.king_james: When their judges are overthrown in stony places, they shall hear my words; for they are sweet.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut crassitudo terræ erupta est super terram,  dissipata sunt ossa nostra secus infernum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּמ֤וֹ פֹלֵ֣חַ וּבֹקֵ֣עַ בָּאָ֑רֶץ נִפְזְר֥וּ עֲ֝צָמֵ֗ינוּ לְפִ֣י שְׁאֽוֹל׃
verses.king_james: Our bones are scattered at the grave’s mouth, as when one cutteth and cleaveth wood upon the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia ad te, Domine, Domine, oculi mei ;  in te speravi, non auferas animam meam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י אֵלֶ֨יךָ ׀ יֱהֹוִ֣ה אֲדֹנָ֣י עֵינָ֑י בְּכָ֥ה חָ֝סִ֗יתִי אַל־תְּעַ֥ר נַפְשִֽׁי׃
verses.king_james: But mine eyes are unto thee, O God the Lord: in thee is my trust; leave not my soul destitute.

verses.latin_vulgate: Custodi me a laqueo quem statuerunt mihi,  et a scandalis operantium iniquitatem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שׇׁמְרֵ֗נִי מִ֣ידֵי פַ֭ח יָ֣קְשׁוּ לִ֑י וּ֝מֹקְשׁ֗וֹת פֹּ֣עֲלֵי אָֽוֶן׃
verses.king_james: Keep me from the snares which they have laid for me, and the gins of the workers of iniquity.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cadent in retiaculo ejus peccatores :  singulariter sum ego, donec transeam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִפְּל֣וּ בְמַכְמֹרָ֣יו רְשָׁעִ֑ים יַ֥חַד אָ֝נֹכִ֗י עַֽד־אֶעֱבֽוֹר׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Let the wicked fall into their own nets, whilst that I withal escape.
